mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and depress equity prices.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered months despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand that reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during periods of high inflation signal supply bottlenecks that trigger rapid risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops during high inflation periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while core inflation remains elevated, real rates increase sharply, tightening financial conditions and generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Rising rates alongside high inflation produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that cause rapid portfolio de-risking and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP contractions are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 70% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low unemployment signals strong economic momentum that drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains during low unemployment periods yield negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases.
